text.title: Session File Resets, Long-Term Files Do Not
text.summary: session.md is the only memory file the agent may rewrite per-session; the other three are append-only across all sessions.

section.title: The session.md exception
section.p: An agent always needs a scratchpad: which task it picked up, which step it is on, which files it has touched. That scratchpad is volatile by definition and would pollute the long-term files. session.md is the exception that proves the append-only rule.

rule.statement: `.aidocs/memory/session.md` MUST be the ONLY memory file an LLM agent rewrites. The agent MUST overwrite `session.md` with the current session's task, step, and modified-files list at every checkpoint. Pre-commit MUST allow non-additive diffs to `session.md` and MUST forbid them on `decisions.md`, `patterns.md`, and `mistakes.md`. At session end (success or abort), if any state in `session.md` should persist, the agent MUST promote it to one of the long-term files via append (with citation), not by leaving it in `session.md`.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit scratchpad agents either pollute long-term memory with per-task state or invent new files ad-hoc; both failure modes are documented in published Cursor and Aider postmortems. The promote-on-end rule keeps the boundary one-way: long-term files only grow, and the only way knowledge enters them is a deliberate, citation-bearing append.

example.code:
# session.md (volatile) ## Active task NERO-412 — pin uv 0.4 across CI
## Step 3/5 — running `pre-commit run --all-files`
## Files touched - pyproject.toml - .github/workflows/ci.yml
## Promote at end - DECISION: pin uv >= 0.4 → decisions.md (cite commit + ticket).
example.note: Volatile state in `session.md`; explicit promote queue keeps long-term files clean.

example.code: # decisions.md (after agent run) ## 2026-04-26 — Working on NERO-412 Currently editing pyproject.toml. Will commit shortly.
example.note: Per-session scratchpad written into the long-term decisions file. Two months later this is noise no reviewer can clean up safely.

antipattern.description: Skipping `session.md` entirely and dumping live state into `patterns.md` or `mistakes.md`.
antipattern.reason: Pollutes the audit log, breaks the append-only invariant, and makes the long-term files untrustworthy for the next agent that loads them.

section.reference: aider conventions — explicit scratch vs persistent file separation.
Cursor memories — comparison case showing why opaque memory is hard to migrate.
